title: draba bagmatiensis
intro: Draba bagmatiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as a wild mustard plant. It is found in the Himalayan region and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cough and cold, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Draba bagmatiensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
